title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
The continuous Fourier transform of a function \(f(t)\) is:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
\]
And the inverse:
\[
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
\]

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
The continuous Fourier transform of a function \(f(t)\) is:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
\]
And the inverse:
\[
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
\]

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Key properties



Property
Time domain
Frequency domain




Linearity
\(\alpha f + \beta g\)
\(\alpha \hat{f} + \beta \hat{g}\)


Time shift
\(f(t - t_0)\)
\(e^{-2\pi i \xi t_0} \hat{f}(\xi)\)


Convolution
\((f * g)(t)\)
\(\hat{f}(\xi) \cdot \hat{g}(\xi)\)


Parseval
\(\int |f|^2\, dt\)
\(\int |\hat{f}|^2\, d\xi\)

Replace images/fourier.gif with your own Manim output. Any .gif or .mp4 in the images/ folder works — no extra config needed.

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Worked example — Gaussian
For \(f(t) = e^{-\pi t^2}\), the Fourier transform is its own Gaussian:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = e^{-\pi \xi^2}
\]
This is the only function (up to scaling) that is an eigenfunction of the Fourier transform with eigenvalue 1.

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Code block (optional)
You can also include executable Python code cells if you have Jupyter installed:

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t = np.linspace(-3, 3, 1000)
f = np.exp(-np.pi * t**2)
xi = np.fft.fftfreq(len(t), d=t[1]-t[0])
fhat = np.fft.fft(f) * (t[1]-t[0])

plt.plot(np.fft.fftshift(xi), np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fhat)))
plt.xlabel(r"$\xi$")
plt.title("Magnitude spectrum")
plt.show()

title: About
text:
This site is built with Quarto and hosted on GitHub Pages.
It’s a place for math notes, Manim animations, and anything else worth writing down.

title: Home
text: Welcome to my site. Here you’ll find math notes, animations, and write-ups.

title: Home
text:
Quick example — inline LaTeX
The quadratic formula is \(x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}\).
Euler’s identity: \(e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0\)

title: Home
text:
Display math
Maxwell’s equations in differential form:
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0}
\]
\[
\nabla \times \mathbf{B} = \mu_0 \mathbf{J} + \mu_0 \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial \mathbf{E}}{\partial t}
\]
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{B}=0
\] \[
\nabla \times \mathbf{E}=-\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{dt}
\]
